name: African Chicken Wings
recipeIngredient: ½ teaspoon cayenne, or to taste
⅓ cup natural style peanut butter
¼ cup canned cream of coconut, well stirred
¼ cup red bell pepper, chopped
⅛ teaspoon dried hot red pepper flakes, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the chicken wings: Mince and mash the garlic and shallots to a paste with the salt.
2. In a large bowl, stir the paste together with the Chinese 5 spice powder, paprika, rosemary, cayenne, and vegetable oil. Mix well.
3. Add the chicken wings. Toss and stir them until they are completely covered with the marinade.
4. Let them marinate, covered and chilled for 4 hours or overnight.
5. Arrange the wings, skin side up, on the rack of a foil-lined large broiler pan and bake in the upper third of a preheated 425°F oven for 25 to 30 minutes or until they are golden.
6. The wings may be prepared one day in advance, kept covered and chilled, and then reheated before serving.
7. To make the sauce: In a blender, blend together the peanut butter, cream of coconut, garlic, water, red bell pepper, red pepper flakes, and soy sauce until the mixture is smooth.
8. Season the sauce with salt to taste.
9. Transfer the sauce to a serving bowl set on a platter.
10. Arrange the wings around the bowl and garnish the platter with coriander sprigs, if desired.
